name: Kenji
observedShift: Kenji is around three months in, and this is a common point where uncertainty and confidence dips show up. I need to understand whether he feels clear on expectations, connected to support, and safe asking for help. Notes suggest he may be carrying frustration about shifting requirements while trying to prove himself. This should stay exploratory, not corrective: I want to hear what feels hardest right now and where our team setup is making the role harder than it needs to be.

focusPoints.label: What feels unclear about expectations right now.
focusPoints.reason: Whether he feels clear on what “good” looks like for his level and what success means in the next few weeks, since this is the three-month dip point.

focusPoints.label: How safe he feels asking for help.
focusPoints.reason: Whether he feels connected to support and safe raising questions or blockers, especially if he’s carrying frustration while trying to prove himself.

focusPoints.label: Shifting requirements — what’s hardest to handle.
focusPoints.reason: Notes suggest frustration with changing requirements; could be the churn itself, unclear decision ownership, or difficulty getting tradeoffs resolved—worth clarifying what part feels hardest right now.

focusPoints.label: Where the team setup is making it harder.
focusPoints.reason: What he wants more of from you that would reduce friction in the daily loop—like faster clarification, clearer ownership, or more structured check-ins—so the role isn’t harder than it needs to be.
